target_user: AI engineers, R&D teams: orchestrating complex multi-agent systems and maintaining consistent context across steps.
reason: Mainstream adoption and refinement of multi-agent frameworks post-conference season requires robust state management.

target_user: Online travel agencies, hospitality groups: real-time, personalized trip planning with dynamic pricing and availability.
reason: Peak summer travel booking season commences in late spring, driving demand for highly contextualized travel recommendations.

target_user: AI solution architects, product managers: showcasing cutting-edge multi-modal AI at major conferences.
reason: Microsoft Build (mid-May) and Google I/O (often May) drive demand for robust multi-modal AI demonstration backends.

target_user: Customer service managers, contact center directors: seamless handover of customer context between AI agents and human agents.
reason: Companies prepare for increased summer support volumes by deploying more sophisticated AI agents requiring better context handoffs.

target_user: Game developers, AI designers: enabling rich, contextual, and memory-aware NPCs that adapt to player actions and game state.
reason: Growing interest in generative AI for games, with new game announcements and development cycles peaking before summer releases.

target_user: Retail operations, supply chain managers: real-time inventory adjustments driven by on-site AI agents for pre-summer sales.
reason: Retailers prepare for summer sales spikes by optimizing inventory with edge AI, needing robust local context management.

reason: Many businesses close new deals before mid-year, driving demand for efficient, personalized sales outreach.
